# SAST baseline (issue #54) — pre-existing clang-tidy findings that do NOT gate.
#
# Format: '<file> <check>' pairs (line numbers deliberately omitted so ordinary
# edits that shift lines don't churn the baseline). The SAST workflow fails on any
# cert-err33-c / clang-analyzer-core.uninitialized.* finding in a (file, check)
# pair NOT listed here — i.e. only NEW findings gate.
#
# Shrink this file as findings get fixed; never grow it without a tribunal-level
# reason. Regenerate a candidate list with:
#   run-clang-tidy -p build-ct src/ -checks='-*,cert-err33-c,clang-analyzer-core.uninitialized.*' \
#     | grep -E 'warning:.*\[' | sed -E 's|^.*/(src/[^:]+):[0-9]+:[0-9]+: warning: .*\[([a-zA-Z0-9._-]+)\]$|\1 \2|' | sort -u
src/build/assemble.c cert-err33-c
src/checker/check_modules.c cert-err33-c
src/checker/collect.c cert-err33-c
src/checker/initanalysis.c cert-err33-c
src/checker/resolve.c cert-err33-c
src/codegen/codegen.c cert-err33-c
src/codegen/codegen.c clang-analyzer-core.uninitialized.Assign
src/driver.c cert-err33-c
src/lexer/lexer.c cert-err33-c
src/parser/cursor.c cert-err33-c
src/runtime/teko_rt.c cert-err33-c
src/vm/vm.c cert-err33-c
